reason: Backend entrypoints and tool contracts may need small diagnostic adjustments, so implementation should start from the backend checklist.
The task must surface unavailable file logging and runtime diagnostics as actionable MCP-facing failures or warnings.
Runtime diagnostics improvements must not pollute MCP stdout and should keep operational logs on stderr only.
Most of the implementation lands in the Godot plugin layer, so plugin-side checklist and conventions apply.
Runtime log cursors, availability state, and scene-run tracking introduce plugin-side state that should remain disciplined.
Plugin-side path resolution and request handling must stay defensive when touching project settings and file I/O.
This task spans plugin log ingestion, MCP tool semantics, and developer debugging workflow, so cross-layer reasoning is required.
